ddc.class.chosen: You are now a %s with %s hit points.
ddc.error.already_driving: You are already possessing something. Sneak to let go.
ddc.error.already_possessed: Someone is already driving that one.
ddc.error.bad_dice: Not a dice expression. Try 1d20+3, 2d6 or 8d6.
ddc.error.book_full: Your book is full. Forget a spell first.
ddc.error.feature_not_yours: Your class does not have that feature.
ddc.error.feature_used: You have used that already. Take a rest.
ddc.error.mode_needs_d20: %s applies only to a single d20 roll.
ddc.error.no_block: Look at a block, or name one.
ddc.error.no_book: You need a spellbook in hand to write in it.
ddc.error.no_class: You have no class yet. Pick one with the wheel or /ddc class <id>.
ddc.error.no_encounters: No data pack defines any encounters.
ddc.error.no_slots: You have no spell slots of that level left. Take a rest.
ddc.error.not_a_creature: You can only cast that at a creature.
ddc.error.not_a_mob: You can only possess a creature with a mind of its own.
ddc.error.not_gm: Only a Game Master can do that.
ddc.error.not_prepared: That spell is not in your spellbook.
ddc.error.out_of_range: That target is out of range.
ddc.error.spell_too_high: You cannot cast a spell of that level yet.
ddc.error.unknown_ability: No such ability: '%s'. Try strength, or DEX.
ddc.error.unknown_class: No loaded data pack defines the class '%s'.
ddc.error.unknown_divinity: No such use of the channel: %s
ddc.error.unknown_encounter: No loaded data pack defines the encounter '%s'.
ddc.error.unknown_entity: That encounter names an entity this server does not have.
ddc.error.unknown_race: No loaded data pack defines the race '%s'.
ddc.error.unknown_spell: No loaded data pack defines the spell '%s'.
ddc.feature.action_surge: Action surge: %s seconds of everything at once
ddc.feature.channel_bless: Channel divinity: %s of you steadied for %s seconds
ddc.feature.channel_heal: Channel divinity: %s hit points to %s of you
ddc.feature.maneuver.parry: Parry: %s damage, and you brace for the next blow
ddc.feature.maneuver.push: Pushing attack: %s damage, and %s is shoved back
ddc.feature.maneuver.trip: Tripping attack: %s damage, and %s goes down
ddc.focus.nothing_to_cast: You have no spells to cast with this.
ddc.gm.mob_died: Your monster died. You are yourself again.
ddc.gm.narrate_only: Only a Game Master can narrate.
ddc.gm.possessing: You are %s. Sneak to let go.
ddc.guide.character.1: Press R and pick a Class: fighter, wizard, cleric or rogue, or whatever a data pack on this server adds.
ddc.guide.character.2: Then pick a Race. It raises an ability, sets your speed, and hands you a starting kit - an elf's bow, a dwarf's axe.
ddc.guide.character.3: Your class decides your hit points, and those are your real hearts: a fighter has more than a wizard, and everyone has more than they did in vanilla.
ddc.guide.character.title: Making a character
ddc.guide.checks.1: Some things are not certain. An iron door asks for a Strength check when you try it; fail and it does not budge.
ddc.guide.checks.2: A Game Master can call for one from anybody: /ddc check dexterity 15. Everyone sees that roll, because everyone is watching you try.
ddc.guide.checks.title: Checks
ddc.guide.combat.1: Hitting something rolls a d20 against its armour class. Miss and the blow does nothing - that is the sweep and the thud you hear.
ddc.guide.combat.2: Only you see your attack rolls. The table sees whether it landed, not the maths.
ddc.guide.combat.3: A fighter has manoeuvres and a surge; a rogue does extra damage from behind. All of it is on the wheel.
ddc.guide.combat.title: Fighting
ddc.guide.crafting.1: Everything DDC adds is made at a crafting table:
ddc.guide.crafting.title: Crafting
ddc.guide.dice.1: The wheel's Roll throws a d20 in front of you. Everyone within 32 blocks sees it land and reads the total.
ddc.guide.dice.2: 20 is a natural 20: the world slows down and the screen turns gold. 1 is a natural 1, and everybody hears about it.
ddc.guide.dice.3: You can roll anything: /roll 2d6+3, or /roll 1d20 advantage. Advantage rolls twice and keeps the better one.
ddc.guide.dice.title: Rolling
ddc.guide.gm.1: A Game Master is any player the server gives Minecraft's gamemaster permission to. The server checks it on every GM action.
ddc.guide.gm.2: Press G for the panel: narrate a scene, turn the day, start a storm, stop the clock, freeze the party.
ddc.guide.gm.3: Narration is letterboxed across everyone's screen. It is the mod's voice for yours.
ddc.guide.gm.title: The Game Master
ddc.guide.gm_wand.1: Hold the GM's wand and press R: your encounters are on the wheel. Picking one only chooses it.
ddc.guide.gm_wand.2: Right-click the ground to place the chosen encounter there. Sneak-right-click cycles which one is next.
ddc.guide.gm_wand.3: Right-click a creature to possess it: you drive it, it hits what you look at, and it is four times tougher. Sneak to let go.
ddc.guide.gm_wand.title: The wand
ddc.guide.gm_world.1: /ddc world day, night, storm, clear - the sky is yours. Stop the clock and it stops for everyone.
ddc.guide.gm_world.2: /roll 1d20 hidden rolls where nobody can see. A GM's dice are their own business.
ddc.guide.gm_world.3: Every class, race, spell and encounter is a JSON file. Write your own and /reload - no code, and no restart.
ddc.guide.gm_world.title: Running the world
ddc.guide.levelling.1: Killing something earns experience worth its hit points. Level up and your hit points, proficiency and spell slots all grow.
ddc.guide.levelling.2: A Game Master can award experience for anything: /ddc xp 300. Talking a guard captain around is worth as much as fighting one.
ddc.guide.levelling.title: Levelling
ddc.guide.magic.1: Craft a spellbook and write spells into it with /ddc prepare. Cantrips need no writing down - you always have them.
ddc.guide.magic.2: A wand casts cantrips, a staff casts anything prepared: hold one and right-click what you are aiming at. Sneak-click changes the spell.
ddc.guide.magic.3: Spells above a cantrip cost a slot. The pips on your HUD are your slots; a rest gives them back.
ddc.guide.magic.title: Casting
ddc.guide.open: New here? Press R and pick Guide, or run /ddc guide.
ddc.guide.rest.1: The wheel's Rest is a long rest: hit points back, spell slots back, once-per-rest features back.
ddc.guide.rest.2: It is not a bed and it does not skip the night. Ask your Game Master when the party may take one.
ddc.guide.rest.title: Resting
ddc.guide.streaming.1: /ddcstream overlay start serves a page for OBS: add it as a Browser Source and your rolls and party appear on the stream.
ddc.guide.streaming.2: /ddcstream twitch connect reads your chat, so viewers can vote !adv or !dis on the next roll.
ddc.guide.streaming.3: Channel points: put DDC_TWITCH_CLIENT_ID and DDC_TWITCH_TOKEN in your environment, then /ddcstream twitch rewards start. Map a reward with /ddcstream twitch reward "Chaos Spawn" ddc spawn ddc:zombie_patrol — a redemption runs a command as you, so it can only do what you could type.
ddc.guide.streaming.title: Streaming
ddc.guide.title: How to play DDC
ddc.guide.welcome.1: DDC turns Minecraft into a tabletop game. You play a character with a class, a race and hit points, and a d20 decides the things that could go either way.
ddc.guide.welcome.2: One person may be the Game Master. They do not survive; they run the world - its monsters, its weather, and its story.
ddc.guide.welcome.3: Press R for your wheel. Everything you can do is on it, so nothing has to be typed. C is your sheet.
ddc.guide.welcome.title: Welcome to the table
ddc.race.chosen: You are now a %s. Speed %s ft.
ddc.race.missing_item: Your race asks for %s, which is not installed.
ddc.recipe.scroll: One spell, once, without preparing it. The scroll burns.
ddc.recipe.spellbook: Holds the spells you prepare. A wizard needs one in hand to write.
ddc.recipe.staff: Casts anything you have prepared. Sneak-click to change the spell.
ddc.recipe.wand: Casts cantrips at what you right-click. Never runs out.
ddc.rest.taken: You take a long rest. Hit points, slots and features restored.
ddc.scroll.unreadable: The writing means nothing to you now.
ddc.spell.cantrip_known: %s is a cantrip: you already know it.
ddc.stream.overlay_running: Overlay running on %s — add that URL to OBS as a Browser Source.
ddc.stream.rewards_failed: Twitch refused the connection. See the log; the token is not in it.
ddc.stream.rewards_none: No rewards are mapped. Try: /ddcstream twitch reward "Chaos Spawn" ddc spawn ddc:zombie_patrol
ddc.stream.rewards_started: Channel points connected. Rewards you have mapped will fire.
ddc.stream.rewards_unconfigured: Set DDC_TWITCH_CLIENT_ID and DDC_TWITCH_TOKEN in your environment first, then restart the game. DDC never asks for a token in chat.
ddc.world.freeze: The party is rooted where they stand.
